description_long: This object is a beige CRT computer monitor with a boxy, angular design typical of late 1990s and early 2000s technology. The front features a glass display screen surrounded by a thick, matte-finished plastic bezel, with several round control buttons neatly arranged along the lower right edge. The sides and rear are solid and robust, with subtle venting and a manufacturer's label on the back panel, as well as a power switch and a small blue indicator light. The monitor sits on a wide, slightly elevated base that provides stability but does not appear to offer height or tilt adjustment. The screen is currently displaying a first-person shooter video game, adding a touch of realism and context to the object. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function over form.
description: A beige CRT computer monitor with a thick plastic bezel, control buttons on the front, and a wide, stable base. The screen displays a video game, and the rear features a manufacturer's label and venting.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass display, thick beige bezel, and a row of round control buttons below the screen.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the monitor's deep, angular profile and solid plastic casing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the manufacturer's label, venting, power switch, and a blue indicator light.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the monitor's angular shape and a yellow caution sticker near the base.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and made of matte beige plastic with slightly rounded corners and no color contrast or decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, flat base integrated into the lower casing, made of the same beige plastic, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A row of round buttons is located on the lower right front bezel, grouped together directly below the screen.
